content: We can reframe the three stages of Three-Stage Progressive Reinforcement Learning as a layered memory prioritization scheme, where each progressive training stage dynamically prunes low-curiosity unexpected state transitions to fit the memory constraints of a memory-efficient ICM implementation. This creates a curious agent that expands its intrinsic reward model only as it masters simpler exploration challenges, avoiding the memory bloat that plagues large-scale autonomous agents running ICM on unfiltered state data.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
